all.en: The marketing department had been arguing for months over priorities and budgets.
all.zh: 行銷部門已經為了優先順序與預算爭執了好幾個月。

all.en: Their director, Karen, booked a three-day team-building retreat at a wooden mountain lodge.
all.zh: 他們的總監 Karen 預訂了一趟為期三天、在一座木造山中小屋的團隊建立活動。

all.en: Twelve colleagues climbed into a chartered minibus on a misty Friday morning.
all.zh: 在一個霧氣朦朧的週五早晨，十二位同事一起爬上了一輛包租的小巴士。

all.en: The road wound through forested hills until the city completely disappeared behind them.
all.zh: 道路蜿蜒穿過林木茂密的山丘，直到城市完全消失在他們身後。

all.en: Phones lost signal halfway up the mountain, which made some people visibly anxious.
all.zh: 手機在上山途中半路就沒了訊號，這讓有些人明顯感到焦慮。

all.en: The lodge owner, an older man named Mr. Lin, greeted them with hot ginger tea.
all.zh: 山莊的主人是一位上了年紀的男士，名叫林先生，他用熱薑茶迎接他們。

all.en: He explained the simple house rules: no work talk before lunch, and no shouting indoors.
all.zh: 他解釋了簡單的家規：午餐前不談公事，在室內不大聲喧嘩。

all.en: The first activity was a silent hike through a quiet bamboo grove.
all.zh: 第一項活動是穿過一片寧靜竹林的靜默健行。

all.en: Karen asked everyone to notice three sounds, three smells, and three colors along the way.
all.zh: Karen 請每個人在路途上留意三種聲音、三種氣味與三種顏色。

all.en: After the walk, the team gathered around a long wooden table for homemade dumplings.
all.zh: 健行結束後，團隊圍坐在一張長長的木桌旁享用手工水餃。

all.en: Even the quietest staff member, a data analyst named Eric, started laughing at shared jokes.
all.zh: 就連團隊裡最安靜的成員——一位名叫 Eric 的資料分析師——都開始為大家共有的笑話而笑了。

all.en: On Saturday morning, Mr. Lin led them in a cooperative rope-bridge exercise across a small stream.
all.zh: 週六早上，林先生帶領他們進行一項合作式的繩橋活動，跨越一條小溪。

all.en: The team had to carry buckets of water without spilling a single drop.
all.zh: 團隊必須提著好幾桶水通過，而且一滴都不能灑出來。

all.en: They failed three times before realizing that listening, not speed, was the actual challenge.
all.zh: 他們失敗了三次，才意識到傾聽而非速度才是真正的挑戰。

all.en: By the fourth attempt, the buckets arrived dry and the team cheered louder than expected.
all.zh: 到了第四次嘗試，水桶完整無損地抵達，團隊歡呼的聲音比預期還大。

all.en: Saturday afternoon was reserved for a difficult but quiet workshop about workplace conflicts.
all.zh: 週六下午保留給一場困難但安靜的工作坊，主題是職場衝突。

all.en: Karen invited everyone to write down one frustration anonymously on a slip of paper.
all.zh: Karen 邀請每個人匿名把一項挫折寫在一張紙條上。

all.en: She read the notes aloud one by one and the room grew thoughtful and respectful.
all.zh: 她一張一張地朗讀那些紙條，整個房間變得既深思又互相尊重。

all.en: A few colleagues admitted feelings they had never shared during normal office hours.
all.zh: 幾位同事坦承了一些他們在平常上班時間從未分享過的感受。

all.en: Eric finally explained why he often went silent during loud meetings.
all.zh: Eric 終於說明了為什麼他在吵雜的會議中常常會沉默。

all.en: Others promised to slow down and to invite quieter voices into the conversation.
all.zh: 其他人答應會放慢步調，並邀請較安靜的聲音加入對話。

all.en: That evening they roasted sweet potatoes over a small bonfire under a bright Milky Way.
all.zh: 那天傍晚他們在一堆小小的營火上烤地瓜，頭頂上是一條明亮的銀河。

all.en: Stories from childhood, college, and first jobs floated between sparks and laughter.
all.zh: 兒時、大學以及第一份工作的故事在火花與笑聲之間飄盪。

all.en: Sunday morning brought a sudden, heavy rain that canceled the planned outdoor archery.
all.zh: 週日早晨突然下起一場大雨，原本計畫的戶外射箭活動因此取消。

all.en: Instead, the team played old-fashioned board games in the lodge's warm common room.
all.zh: 於是團隊改在山莊溫暖的公共空間裡玩起了老式桌遊。

all.en: Karen noticed that even the rivalries from the office had turned into friendly teasing.
all.zh: Karen 注意到連辦公室裡的競爭關係也都變成了友善的鬥嘴。

all.en: Before lunch, Mr. Lin asked each guest to write a one-sentence wish for the team's future.
all.zh: 午餐前，林先生請每位客人為團隊的未來寫下一句願望。

all.en: He sealed the slips in a glass jar and promised to mail it back in six months.
all.zh: 他把紙條封進一個玻璃罐裡，並承諾六個月後寄回去給他們。

all.en: On the bus ride home, the energy felt completely different from the trip up.
all.zh: 在回程的巴士上，整個氛圍與上山時感覺完全不同。

all.en: People exchanged personal phone numbers and shared playlists for the long descent.
all.zh: 大家在漫長的下山路上互相交換了私人電話並分享了播放清單。

all.en: Back at the office on Monday, the morning meeting started with unexpected gentleness.
all.zh: 週一回到辦公室後，晨會以一種出乎意料的溫和方式開始。

all.en: Karen used new turn-taking rules so that every voice could finish without interruption.
all.zh: Karen 採用了新的輪流發言規則，讓每個人的發言都能不被打斷地說完。

all.en: Six months later the glass jar arrived, just as Mr. Lin had promised.
all.zh: 六個月後玻璃罐如期寄到，就如同林先生承諾的那樣。

all.en: Half of the wishes had quietly come true, and the team read them with shy pride.
all.zh: 其中一半的願望已悄悄實現，團隊帶著靦腆的驕傲讀著它們。

all.en: From then on, the mountain lodge weekend was simply called the trip that changed things.
all.zh: 從那時起，那次山莊週末就被簡單稱為改變了一切的那次旅程。
